sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Patient reports an iron deficiency anemia and difficulty donating blood.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a longstanding history of iron deficiency anemia, diagnosed at age 13. She states she has been unable to donate blood recently, around December, and is seeking evaluation for this persistent issue. She describes experiencing fatigue, feverishness with chills, and headaches. She also reports experiencing chills and cold intolerance, frequently needing to bundle up. Additionally, she notes worsening anxiety and depression symptoms. Her last colonoscopy was approximately five to six years ago, and it was unremarkable. She takes ferrous sulfate 25mg twice daily and vitamin B12 over the counter.
Findings
Physical examination reveals no significant findings. The patient denies any tenderness upon palpation. Vital signs are within normal limits. No pacemaker or defibrillator is present. The patient denies alcohol use beyond occasional consumption.
Assessment
The patient presents with persistent iron deficiency anemia, likely exacerbated by underlying factors. Further investigation is warranted to determine the etiology and guide appropriate management.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Order an upper endoscopy and colonoscopy to evaluate for sources of bleeding. A referral to a hematologist has been sent. Initiate iron supplementation with ferrous sulfate, recommending concurrent intake with orange juice to enhance absorption. Schedule a follow-up in four weeks to reassess blood work and monitor response to treatment. A pill camera will be used for capsule endoscopy if initial investigations are inconclusive.
